title: arthrocnemum perenne var. radicans
intro: Arthrocnemum perenne var. radicans, also known as pickleweed, is a succulent shrub that belongs to the Chenopodiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Arthrocnemum perenne var. radicans include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Herbal preparations of pickleweed can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
